1. A **regional geological map** (1:200,000 scale) — the main upper portion
2. Two **geological cross-sections** (геологические разрезы) below the map — structural profiles along two perpendicular transect lines

  - **В–Г** (labeled "РАЗРЕЗ ПО ЛИНИИ ВГ") — a roughly perpendicular transect

## Key Content

### Geological Map (upper panel):
The map displays an exceptionally complex, multiply deformed geological terrane typical of the **Central Kazakhstan fold belt (Kazakh Shield / Saryarka)**. Key observations:

- **Dominant lithological units** are rendered in warm tones (oranges, pinks, reds, tans), suggesting widespread **Paleozoic intrusive and metamorphic rocks** — likely granites, granodiorites, and gneisses — which dominate the central and northern portions of the map
- **Blue and blue-grey units** in the south and southwest suggest **sedimentary or volcanic sequences**, possibly Carboniferous or Devonian age clastic/volcanic rocks
- **Yellow and pale units** in the eastern portion (with horizontal ruling/hatching) indicate **Mesozoic–Cenozoic sedimentary cover** (likely Cretaceous–Neogene), overlying the basement unconformably
- **Pink/magenta elongate bodies** in the south-central area are likely **late-stage granitoid intrusions** (possibly Permian)
- **Structural complexity** is extreme: tightly packed contour lines and fold axial traces indicate significant topographic relief and folded/faulted stratigraphy; numerous **anticlinal and synclinal fold closures** are visible as oval or elongated closed-contour patterns across the central map area
- **Faults** are indicated by bold lines, some with tick marks suggesting thrust or normal fault geometry; a major **NW-SE trending structural lineament** is apparent across the map
- **Mineral occurrence symbols** (small crosses, triangles, or circles) are scattered across the map, consistent with marked **ore occurrences or prospect points**
- A **white/stippled area** in the center-left likely marks a settlement or quarry zone
- **Borehole locations** (скважины) are marked at several points, particularly along the cross-section lines A′–Б and В–Г

### Cross-Section А′–Б (middle panel):
- Vertical scale on left: **ΔT (нТл)** — a **magnetic anomaly curve** plotted at the top of the section, with values ranging approximately from **−100 to +3 nT** (relative units)
- A second curve labeled **ΔT** and **ΔG** suggests **combined magnetic and gravity profiles** are superimposed above the structural section
- The geological section itself shows:
  - Flat-lying to gently dipping **Mesozoic–Cenozoic sedimentary cover** at surface
  - Underlying **folded Paleozoic basement** with multiple thrust faults and fold structures
  - **Depth range:** approximately 0 to **−4000 m** (4 km depth)
  - Numerous **borehole intersections** are marked along the section with formation labels (e.g., Г-7, Г-8, Г-9, Г-10, Г-18, Г-25 and others — likely borehole/drill-hole identifiers)
  - A prominent **pink/magenta layer** near the base likely represents **crystalline basement or a major intrusive body**
  - **Faults** cutting through multiple horizons are shown as inclined lines

### Cross-Section В–Г (lower panel):
- Similar structure to А′–Б but oriented differently (approximately N–S)
- **ΔT (нТл)** magnetic profile at top again, with a prominent **positive anomaly** visible
- Geological section shows:
  - Strongly deformed **Paleozoic units** dipping consistently, suggesting a **major fold or thrust structure**
  - **Blue/teal colored units** at depth (>2000 m) represent older basement sequences
  - **Pink band** at the base again — likely the same crystalline basement
  - Depth range: approximately **0 to −3500 m** with axis labels showing **−1000, −2000, −3000 m**
  - Formation symbols include: С₁, С₂, D, О, Є (Carboniferous, Devonian, Ordovician, Cambrian systems) and possibly Permian intrusions
  - Several **deep boreholes** intersect the section, labeled with Г- or P- prefixed identifiers
